data: Marble plaque (Manganaro describes as veined Luna marble), intact but slightly chipped around the edges.
The text fills the surface, within a simple incised border, with a fixed margin on the left, uneven margin on the right. The space below the text is filled by three staurograms.
Lunate epsilon, sigma and omega, cursive mu; use of supralines on numerals and some abbreviation marks. Manganaro does not record letter heights.

Said to come from the via Androne necropolis, first reported by Libertini from an apograph; said by Manganaro to be in the possession of avv. S. Majorana, found in the area of his house on via Androne; seen and photographed by Manganaro.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana: scan of Manganaro 1959: tav.41
Guido Libertini, «Miscellanea epigrafica», Archivio Storico per la Sicilia Orientale 27 (1931): 39–53

Giacomo Manganaro, «Iscrizioni tardo-imperiali di Catania», in Atti del terzo congresso internazionale di epigrafia greca e latina (Roma 4-8 settembre 1957) (Rome: «L’Erma» di Bretschneider, 1959), 347–51
Le iscrizioni datate della Sicilia paleocristiana
Antonio Ferrua, «Le iscrizioni datate della Sicilia paleocristiana», Kokalos 28–29 (1983 1982): 3–29
Trois aspects de l’influence du latin sur le grec tardif
Denis Feissel, « Trois aspects de l’influence du latin sur le grec tardif », Travaux et mémoires du Centre de recherches d’histoire et civilisation byzantines 8 (1981): 135‑50
Robert Crellin integrated lemmatizations into EpiDoc
Ferrua, 1982-1983, Le iscrizioni datate della Sicilia paleocristiana
Feissel, 1981, Trois aspects de l’influence du latin sur le grec tardif

5ζήσας ἔτη ν´ πλῖο(ν) ἥτ-

9ἰρήνη τῇ ψυχῇ σου
